findings.evidence: `rg` shows `theorem Contractable.map` at line 59; the new declarations at lines 97-121 are proved only by `(h.map hk₁).trans ...` or `h.map fun ...`.
findings.fix: Drop these exported declarations or justify them with actual downstream use; otherwise use `h.map` at call sites.
findings.issue: `Contractable.allStrictMono_eq`, `shift_segment_eq`, `shift_and_select`, and `restrict` are exported corollaries reprovable by immediate calls to the existing `Contractable.map`.

findings.evidence: Mathlib has `theorem Perm.exists_extending_pair` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Logic/Equiv/Fintype.lean:158`; the new theorem at lines 130-140 is exactly an application of it to `Fin.castLE hmn` and `fun i => ⟨k i, hk_bound i⟩`.
findings.fix: Use `Equiv.Perm.exists_extending_pair` directly in `contractable_of_exchangeable`, or keep the wrapper private/local to the proof.
findings.issue: `exists_perm_extending_strictMono` is a public thin wrapper around Mathlib's existing permutation extension API, adding a parallel TauCeti combinatorics lemma.

summary: The main theorem does not duplicate an existing Exchangeability result, but the PR exports helper APIs that are just specializations/wrappers of existing lemmas. Remove or make these local unless a downstream use needs the named specialization.
